why_invalidated: 259 counted ALL diplomatic stream tokens (syllabograms + logograms + fractions + damaged/composite word-internal variants); a phonetic reading's parameters are the SYLLABARY = 88-92 (deduplicated) / 131 (raw) / 123 (allograph families). And '212' counts ≥4-sign non-dominant-site INSCRIPTIONS, which are structural/segmental units, not value-bearing constraints. The two figures are different categories.
corrected_diagnosis: At the SYLLABIC parameter level the count is FAVOURABLE (92 params < 212 structural constraints). The underdetermination is NOT a count deficit — it is that the abundant structural constraints (segmentation, formula grammar, 212 inscriptions) DO NOT INFORM sign VALUES (the distributional value-channel is null 4x; anchors are one-deep). Value-informative constraints (~2) are far below the ~92 syllabic parameters. The ceiling is constraint INFORMATIVENESS at the value layer, not constraint COUNT.

reopen_target.note: The campaign target is NOT 'more constraints to beat a count' (the count already favours a reading) but 'constraints that INFORM the ~92 syllabic values' — i.e. secure external anchors surviving held-out + non-circular, OR an internal channel that transfers to values. Structural/segmental gains (Stages B/D) reduce false parameters and sharpen the space but cannot alone assign values; value anchors (Stage F) are the load-bearing lever.

verdict_reason: The prior UNDERDETERMINED CONCLUSION stands qualitatively, but its quantitative justification (259>212) is INVALIDATED as a category error. Reframed correctly, the question reopens with a sharp, actionable target: value-informative constraints for ~92 syllabic parameters. Continue to Stages B-F to seek them.
